plan: Create `src/ui/clipboard.ts` — a small utility module that wraps `navigator.clipboard.writeText()` with feature detection. Export `copyToClipboard(text: string): Promise<boolean>` that returns `true` on success, `false` when the Clipboard API is unavailable or the write fails. No fallback hacks (execCommand) — keep it honest per the ticket's 'unavailable feedback' acceptance criterion.

Create `src/modules/fingerprint/format-receipt-text.ts` — export `formatReceiptText(title: string, rows: ReceiptRow[]): string` that serialises the receipt into a clean plain-text block. Format: title header line, separator, then each row as `Label: Value`, one per line. Reuse the existing `ReceiptRow` type from `src/ui/receipt.ts`.

Create `src/ui/copy-button.ts` — factory function `createCopyButton(getText: () => string): HTMLButtonElement`. Follow the existing `createRerunButton` pattern: classes `btn btn-secondary receipt-copy-btn`, default label 'Copy', success label 'Copied!' (auto-revert after ~2 s), unavailable label 'Copy unavailable' when Clipboard API is missing. Internally calls `copyToClipboard()` from the clipboard utility. Add `aria-label` for accessibility.

Add styles for the copy button feedback states in `src/styles/receipt.css` — add `.receipt-copy-btn--success` (muted green background flash) and `.receipt-copy-btn--unavailable` (muted warning style). Respect `prefers-reduced-motion` by skipping transitions when reduced motion is active. Keep styling consistent with the existing `.receipt-rerun-btn--loading` pattern.

Integrate the copy button into `src/modules/fingerprint/fingerprint-receipt.ts` — import `createCopyButton` and `formatReceiptText`, build the text callback from the already-computed `rows` array (core + posture rows), create the button, and prepend it to the existing `.receipt-actions` container so it appears before the Re-run Audit button.

Create `tests/us-016/clipboard.test.ts` — unit tests for the clipboard utility: (a) resolves true when writeText succeeds, (b) resolves false when Clipboard API is absent, (c) resolves false when writeText rejects. Stub `navigator.clipboard` via `vi.stubGlobal`.

Create `tests/us-016/format-receipt-text.test.ts` — unit tests for the text formatter: (a) produces expected multi-line string from sample rows, (b) handles empty rows array, (c) includes title in output.

Create `tests/us-016/copy-button.test.ts` — DOM + behaviour tests following the rerun-button test pattern: (a) renders with correct classes and label, (b) calls getText and copyToClipboard on click, (c) shows 'Copied!' feedback on success and reverts, (d) shows 'Copy unavailable' when clipboard is not supported, (e) button is disabled during the copy operation to prevent double-clicks.
Run `npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm run test` to verify all checks pass with no regressions.
testing_recommendations: Unit test clipboard utility with stubbed navigator.clipboard — cover success, API-missing, and rejection paths
Unit test formatReceiptText with various row inputs including edge cases (empty rows, long values, special characters)
DOM tests for copy button: verify element structure, CSS classes, aria-label, click handler wiring
Behaviour tests for copy button: verify success feedback ('Copied!') appears and auto-reverts after timeout, verify 'Copy unavailable' state when Clipboard API is absent
Integration test: verify copy button appears in the receipt-actions container alongside the rerun button
Run full suite: npm run lint && npm run typecheck && npm run test before marking complete
Manual browser test: verify clipboard write works in Chrome/Firefox/Safari, and that unsupported-browser feedback displays correctly
risks: Clipboard API requires secure context (HTTPS or localhost) — copying will fail on plain HTTP deployments; the unavailable feedback path covers this but it should be documented
Timer-based feedback revert (setTimeout for 'Copied!' → 'Copy') can cause flaky tests if not handled with fake timers (vi.useFakeTimers)
Some browsers may prompt the user for clipboard permission, which could be confused with a failure — the utility should treat permission denial as a graceful failure returning false
